current: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas the derived plain-clipped-AIPW exponent. Moreover r_feas<=r_star, with equality at gamma=0 and more generally whenever kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma). The strict positive gap occurs precisely in the nuisance-limited weak-overlap branch kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
proposed: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas=min{r_star,g(a,c,gamma)} as derived in def:feasible-rate. Moreover r_feas<=r_star, with equality exactly when g(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma). At gamma=0, r_feas=min{(1+alpha)/(2+alpha),c}, so equality with r_star holds iff c>=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). The strict positive gap occurs precisely when g(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
reason: After the round-2 drift correction, the nuisance product rate enters additively. Thus at gamma=0 the feasible exponent is min{A_alpha,c}, not unconditionally A_alpha. The original unconditional equality-at-gamma=0 clause is false when c<A_alpha; replacing the stale kappa zeta phrase by the derived g and adding the c>=A_alpha side condition is the minimal narrowing.
